skill.name: interview
skill.tagline: Settle every unknown by the right oracle — research, ask, or experiment.
skill.shortSummary: Routes every open unknown to its correct source: research for facts, ask the user for preferences, experiment for the untried.
skill.fullSummary: Interview classifies every unknown as WORLD-owned (researchable fact), HUMAN-owned (user preference), or NOBODY-owned (unexplored). It eagerly launches parallel research for facts, asks the user a single decision-changing question at a time with a reasoned recommendation, and dispatches experiments for the untried — so discussions settle on evidence and rulings instead of assumptions.

highlights.title: Three-Oracle Routing
highlights.description: Every unknown is classified as WORLD-owned, HUMAN-owned, or NOBODY-owned — facts are researched, preferences are asked, experiments are run. Never assume, never guess.

highlights.title: Eager Parallel Research
highlights.description: The moment a WORLD-owned unknown is identified, multiple background research subagents launch in parallel. Each returns a compact validated brief — saving turns and keeping the conversation moving.

highlights.title: Precise Human Interview
highlights.description: Ask exactly one decision-changing question at a time, always with a recommended answer and reasoning. Batch up to 4 independent forks in one call. Ration the ~4–6 questions per session on what truly changes outcomes.

highlights.title: Explicit Ruling Carry-Forward
highlights.description: Every settled answer is stated explicitly and carried into the current artifact — the plan, document, or code it unblocks. An implicit ruling is not settled.

steps.name: Classify Unknown
steps.description: Label each open unknown as WORLD, HUMAN, or NOBODY.
steps.details: Determine whether the answer lives in researchable fact, the user's head, or the unexplored. This classification drives everything that follows.

steps.name: Research WORLD-owned
steps.description: Launch parallel Explore subagents with WebSearch.
steps.details: For every WORLD-owned unknown, dispatch one background subagent per candidate in a single message. Validate briefs for source credibility, dates, and conflict resolution.

steps.name: Interview HUMAN-owned
steps.description: Ask one question at a time with a reasoned recommendation.
steps.details: Pick the single most decision-changing HUMAN-owned unknown. Use AskUserQuestion with your recommended answer. Ration to ~4–6 questions per session; batch independent forks.

steps.name: Run Experiments
steps.description: Dispatch the attune:experiment skill for NOBODY-owned unknowns.
steps.details: When no one knows the answer until it is tried, launch an experiment. Its outcome returns as evidence for the user to rule on.

steps.name: Carry Ruling Forward
steps.description: State the settled answer and apply it to the current artifact.
steps.details: Before asking the next question, explicitly state the ruling and carry it into the work it unblocks — the active plan, document, or code. An implicit ruling is not settled.
